name: Asia (Lung Cancer) Network
source_paper: Lauritzen, S.L. & Spiegelhalter, D.J. (1988). Local Computations with Probabilities on Graphical Structures and Their Application to Expert Systems. Journal of the Royal Statistical Society B, 50(2), 157-224.
description: A small synthetic Bayesian network for diagnosing lung diseases. Models the relationship between smoking, lung cancer, tuberculosis, bronchitis, and diagnostic test results. Classic benchmark for causal discovery algorithms due to its small size and well-known structure.

variables.name: asia
variables.description: Whether patient has recently visited Asia

variables.name: either
variables.description: Whether patient has either tuberculosis or lung cancer (deterministic OR of tub and lung)

variables.name: dysp
variables.description: Whether patient experiences shortness of breath

edge_format: [source, target] indicating source -> target causal direction
notes: Data generated synthetically from the known DAG structure and conditional probability tables as specified in the original paper. The 'either' node is a deterministic OR gate of 'tub' and 'lung'.
